description: Major national employers known to hire people with felony convictions

employers.name: Amazon
employers.convictionPolicy: Case-by-case review. Does not ask about convictions on application. Background check after conditional offer.
restrictions: No sex offenses for delivery roles
No theft convictions for warehouse within 7 years
employers.benefits: Health insurance day 1, 401k, tuition assistance (Career Choice program)
employers.notes: Largest private employer in the US. Hires hundreds of thousands annually. Very felon-friendly.

employers.name: Walmart
employers.convictionPolicy: Removed conviction question from application in 2018. Individual assessment after background check.
restrictions: No violent felony within 7 years for customer-facing roles
employers.benefits: Health insurance, 401k match, $1/day college degree program (Live Better U)
employers.notes: Second largest employer in the US. Major second-chance employer.

employers.name: FedEx Ground
employers.convictionPolicy: Considers applicants with criminal records. Background check conducted after offer.
restrictions: No DUI within 3 years for driving positions
employers.benefits: Tuition reimbursement, health insurance, paid time off
employers.notes: Especially good for night shift package handlers — high demand, fast hire.

employers.name: UPS
employers.convictionPolicy: Fair chance employer. Does not ask about convictions on application.
employers.benefits: Teamsters union, full health/dental/vision after 1 year, tuition assistance ($25K lifetime)
employers.notes: Union job — strong protections and benefits. Peak season (Oct-Jan) hires heavily.

employers.name: Home Depot
employers.convictionPolicy: Considers criminal history on case-by-case basis after conditional offer.
restrictions: Theft convictions reviewed for cashier/sales positions
employers.benefits: Employee stock purchase, tuition reimbursement, health insurance
employers.notes: Trades knowledge is valued. If you have construction/trades experience, mention it.

employers.name: Waste Management
employers.convictionPolicy: Actively recruits returning citizens through reentry partnerships.
restrictions: Valid CDL required for driver positions
No DUI within 5 years for drivers
employers.benefits: CDL training available, health insurance, 401k, overtime opportunities
employers.notes: One of the most felon-friendly companies. Partners with reentry organizations. CDL drivers in high demand.

employers.name: Tyson Foods
employers.convictionPolicy: Fair chance employer. Considers all applicants regardless of criminal history.
employers.locations: 25+ states (major presence in AR, TN, GA, AL, NC)
employers.benefits: Free ESL classes, citizenship assistance, health insurance, tuition reimbursement
employers.notes: Very felon-friendly. Many production facilities in rural areas. Overtime common.

employers.name: Koch Industries / Georgia-Pacific
employers.convictionPolicy: Koch Industries is a Ban the Box leader. Removed conviction question in 2015.
employers.locations: Nationwide (Georgia-Pacific has mills in GA, TN, and throughout Southeast)
employers.benefits: Competitive pay, health insurance, 401k, profit sharing
employers.notes: Charles Koch is a vocal advocate for second-chance hiring. Company has formal reentry program.

employers.name: Goodwill Industries
employers.convictionPolicy: Mission is to help people overcome barriers to employment. Actively hires returning citizens.
employers.benefits: Job training, career coaching, GED assistance, interview prep
employers.notes: Best 'first job' after release. They EXPECT to hire returning citizens. Also provides free job training.

employers.name: Salvation Army
employers.convictionPolicy: Mission-driven hiring. Actively works with justice-involved populations.
restrictions: Sex offense restrictions for youth-serving roles only
employers.benefits: Supportive environment, potential for housing assistance through programs
employers.notes: Many locations also provide emergency services (food, shelter) to returning citizens.

employers.name: Dave's Hot Chicken
employers.convictionPolicy: Proud second-chance employer. Partners with Homeboy Industries.
employers.benefits: Flexible scheduling, meal discounts, advancement opportunities
employers.notes: Founded with second-chance hiring as core value. Fast-growing chain.

employers.name: Construction / Trades (General)
employers.convictionPolicy: Construction industry is the #1 employer of returning citizens. Most companies don't run background checks for labor positions.
restrictions: Government contract jobs may require clearance
employers.applyUrl: Search '[your city] construction jobs' or visit local union hall
employers.benefits: High demand, overtime, union apprenticeship programs lead to $50-80K+ careers
employers.notes: BEST path for returning citizens with physical ability. Unions (IBEW, UA, Carpenters) have formal apprenticeship programs. Many states have pre-apprenticeship programs specifically for returning citizens.

employers.name: Staffing Agencies (Temp-to-Perm)
employers.convictionPolicy: Many staffing agencies specialize in placing people with records. They handle the background check conversation with the employer.
employers.applyUrl: Search: Kelly Services, Manpower, PeopleReady, Staffmark, Aerotek
employers.benefits: Fast placement (often same week), try before committing, multiple options
employers.notes: PeopleReady is especially felon-friendly — they have a same-day pay app. Walk into any branch with ID.
